en: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "If anyone sins and commits a breach of faith against the LORD by deceiving his neighbor in a matter of deposit or security, or through robbery, or if he has oppressed his neighbor
zh: “如果有人犯罪，對耶和華不忠實，在人寄存給他的物上或抵押品上欺騙自己的同伴，或是搶奪，或是欺壓自己的同胞，

en: or has found something lost and lied about it, swearing falsely- in any of all the things that people do and sin thereby-
zh: 或是拾去人遺失的東西，卻欺騙人，起假誓，行了任何一件人常犯的罪，

en: if he has sinned and has realized his guilt and will restore what he took by robbery or what he got by oppression or the deposit that was committed to him or the lost thing that he found
zh: 他犯了罪，有了罪，就應歸還他所搶奪的，或是欺壓得來的，或是別人寄存在他那裡的，或是他拾去的失物，

en: or anything about which he has sworn falsely, he shall restore it in full and shall add a fifth to it, and give it to him to whom it belongs on the day he realizes his guilt.
zh: 或是他因起假誓所得的任何東西；在賠過的時候，他除了按原數償還外，必須另外加上五分之一，交給原主；

en: And he shall bring to the priest as his compensation to the LORD a ram without blemish out of the flock, or its equivalent for a guilt offering.
zh: 還要照著你的估價，把贖愆祭牲，就是從羊群中取一隻沒有殘疾的公綿羊，牽到耶和華面前，給祭司作贖愆祭。

en: And the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD, and he shall be forgiven for any of the things that one may do and thereby become guilty."
zh: 祭司在耶和華面前，為他所行的任何一件過失贖罪，他就蒙赦免。”

en: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "Command Aaron and his sons, saying, This is the law of the burnt offering. The burnt offering shall be on the hearth on the altar all night until the morning, and the fire of the altar shall be kept burning on it.
zh: “你要吩咐亞倫和他的子孫說：燔祭的律例是這樣：燔祭要放在祭壇的柴上，通宵達旦；祭壇上的火，必須一直燒著。

en: And the priest shall put on his linen garment and put his linen undergarment on his body, and he shall take up the ashes to which the fire has reduced the burnt offering on the altar and put them beside the altar.
zh: 祭司要穿上細麻布外袍，又把細麻布褲子穿在身上；他要把祭壇上燔祭燒成的灰收起來，放在祭壇旁邊；

en: Then he shall take off his garments and put on other garments and carry the ashes outside the camp to a clean place.
zh: 然後脫去這些衣服，穿上別的衣服，把灰拿出營外潔淨的地方。

en: The fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it; it shall not go out. The priest shall burn wood on it every morning, and he shall arrange the burnt offering on it and shall burn on it the fat of the peace offerings.
zh: 祭壇上的火，應常常燒著，不可熄滅；每天早晨，祭司要在上面燒柴，把燔祭排列在壇上，又在上面焚燒平安祭的脂肪。

en: Fire shall be kept burning on the altar continually; it shall not go out.
zh: 火必須不斷在祭壇上燒著，不可熄滅。

en: "And this is the law of the grain offering. The sons of Aaron shall offer it before the LORD in front of the altar.
zh: “素祭的律例是這樣：亞倫的子孫要在耶和華面前的祭壇前獻素祭。

en: And one shall take from it a handful of the fine flour of the grain offering and its oil and all the frankincense that is on the grain offering and burn this as its memorial portion on the altar, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
zh: 從其中取出一把作素祭的細麵、油，和在素祭上所有的乳香，焚燒在祭壇上獻給耶和華為馨香的祭，作供物的象徵。

en: And the rest of it Aaron and his sons shall eat. It shall be eaten unleavened in a holy place. In the court of the tent of meeting they shall eat it.
zh: 亞倫和他的子孫要吃剩餘的祭物，在聖潔的地方作無酵餅吃；他們要在會幕的院子裡吃。

en: It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their portion of my food offerings. It is a thing most holy, like the sin offering and the guilt offering.
zh: 烤餅的時候，不可加酵。這是我從獻給我的火祭中，賜給他們的分，是至聖的，與贖罪祭和贖愆祭一樣。

en: Every male among the children of Aaron may eat of it, as decreed forever throughout your generations, from the LORD's food offerings. Whatever touches them shall become holy."
zh: 亞倫的子孫中的男丁，都要從獻給耶和華的火祭中吃這些祭物；這是你們世世代代永遠的律例。人接觸過這些祭物，必成為聖。”

en: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "This is the offering that Aaron and his sons shall offer to the LORD on the day when he is anointed: a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a regular grain offering, half of it in the morning and half in the evening.
zh: “亞倫和他的子孫，在受膏的日子要獻給耶和華的供物，就是一公斤的細麵，作常獻的素祭，早晨獻一半，晚上獻一半。

en: It shall be made with oil on a griddle. You shall bring it well mixed, in baked pieces like a grain offering, and offer it for a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
zh: 你要把這些細麵在煎盤上用油調和作餅，烘 好了，你就拿進來；將擘成碎塊的素祭獻上，獻給耶和華為馨香的祭。

en: The priest from among Aaron's sons, who is anointed to succeed him, shall offer it to the LORD as decreed forever. The whole of it shall be burned.
zh: 亞倫的子孫繼承他作受膏立的祭司的，都要獻這素祭，把它完全焚燒，獻給耶和華，作永遠的律例。

en: Every grain offering of a priest shall be wholly burned. It shall not be eaten."
zh: 凡是祭司獻的素祭都要完全焚燒，不可以吃。”

en: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying, This is the law of the sin offering. In the place where the burnt offering is killed shall the sin offering be killed before the LORD; it is most holy.
zh: “你要告訴亞倫和他的兒子：贖罪祭的律例是這樣：贖罪祭牲應在宰殺燔祭牲的地方，在耶和華面前宰殺，這是至聖的。

en: The priest who offers it for sin shall eat it. In a holy place it shall be eaten, in the court of the tent of meeting.
zh: 獻這贖罪祭的祭司要吃這祭牲；在會幕院子裡的聖潔地方吃。

en: Whatever touches its flesh shall be holy, and when any of its blood is splashed on a garment, you shall wash that on which it was splashed in a holy place.
zh: 接觸過這祭肉的，必成為聖。沾了祭牲的血，必須清理；血濺在衣服上，應該在聖潔的地方洗淨，

en: And the earthenware vessel in which it is boiled shall be broken. But if it is boiled in a bronze vessel, that shall be scoured and rinsed in water.
zh: 煮祭物的瓦器卻要打碎；如果煮祭物的是銅器，只需用水沖洗。

en: Every male among the priests may eat of it; it is most holy.
zh: 祭司家中的男丁，都可以吃祭肉；這是至聖的。

en: But no sin offering shall be eaten from which any blood is brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the Holy Place; it shall be burned up with fire.
zh: 但是任何贖罪祭，如果它的血帶進了會幕在聖所內進行贖罪，祭肉就不可吃；必須用火燒盡。”
